rationale.R1.6: 'Clean this up' on a prose artifact canonically targets writing polish: sentence clarity, elimination of redundancy, flow between paragraphs, and register consistency. The artifact has several candidates — e.g., the abrupt tense shift in the third paragraph ('We train…') and the repetitive 'routing-specific supervision' phrasing — making this the load-bearing dimension. R1.5 sits at 0.3 (peripheral) because light structural tidying can accompany polish, but the delegation contains no signal for deliberate reorganization.
rationale.RX.1: Any edits must not suppress or paper over the self-contradicting training-free / trained-on-RouterBench tension already present in the artifact; uncertainty about that claim must remain visible.
rationale.RX.2: Polish must not amplify the overclaim that EcoRoute is 'training-free' when the scoring head is in fact trained; edits should not strengthen language that obscures this.
rationale.RX.3: Scope is writing quality on the supplied section only; no content should be added, removed, or reframed beyond what serves clarity.
rationale.RX.4: The artifact makes performance and cost claims referenced to RouterBench; edits must not alter those figures or the framing of comparisons.
rationale.RX.5: Any rephrasing of cited systems (FrugalGPT, RouteLLM, RouterBench) must preserve accurate attribution and not misrepresent their described behavior.
